page.title: Complex Relative Clauses
page.subtitle_html: Complex Relative Clauses - C1 relative clauses with whose, prepositions, embedded information, and non-defining detail.
page.subtitle_text: C1 relative clauses with whose, prepositions, embedded information, and non-defining detail.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Complex Relative Clauses: compact anchors for precise C1 relative clauses.","rules":[{"label":"Whose","text":"Use whose to connect ownership or relationship without repeating the noun.","example":"The company whose reputation had suffered issued an apology."},{"label":"Prepositions","text":"In formal style, a preposition can stand before whom or which.","example":"The issue about which we disagreed has been resolved."},{"label":"Non-defining detail","text":"Use commas when the clause adds extra, non-essential information.","example":"The proposal, which many experts supported, was approved."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English C1 complex relative clauses lesson package.
